mappings.rationale: Directly covers the historical development of medical education, practice, and public health services.
Directly covers the ethical basis of medical practice (section C.2.a) including issues of euthanasia, abortion, and the Hippocratic oath.
Covers licensure requirements, legal restrictions on practice (sections C.2.b, C.2.c), and the legal framework of healthcare delivery.
Directly covers public health services and administration (section D), vaccination and immunization, and epidemic prevention and control.
Covers the scientific methods underlying public health services, disease surveillance, and the prevention and control of epidemics (section H.5).
Directly relevant to the prevention and control of epidemics (section H.5) and quarantine and isolation of infected victims (section H.2).
Relevant to the control of foods and drugs (section I.2), prophylactic medication (section H.4), and therapeutic agent use.
Relevant to prevention and control of infection (section H), including vaccination, quarantine, and destruction of infectious agents.
Directly relevant to prevention of malnutrition (section G), including nutrient requirements and problems of world food supply.
A major global public health challenge requiring coordinated prevention, control, and treatment programs across healthcare systems.
Illustrates historical approaches to epidemic prevention and control (section H.5) including quarantine and public health responses.
A field of specialized medical practice (section B) with specific hospital and specialist practice considerations.
A key field of specialized hospital and specialist practice (section C.1.b) essential to modern surgery.
A related discipline to medical practice, relevant to governmental practice and legal aspects of medicine.
A related discipline connecting medicine and law, relevant to specialized medical research and practice.
A related discipline to medical practice, relevant to understanding zoonotic disease control and one-health approaches to infection prevention.
Relevant to environmental sanitation and health (section F), including the economics of air, water, and soil pollution control.
Relevant to environmental sanitation and health (section F) and political dimensions of pollution control.
Relevant to environmental sanitation (section F) and health and safety laws (section I.2) governing pollution control.
Relevant to the control of foods (section I.2) and efforts to address world food supply problems (section G).
Relevant to understanding world population in relation to malnutrition prevention (section G) and public health service planning.
Relevant to the wide variation in medical practice among countries (section C.1) and global health challenges including epidemics and malnutrition.
Relevant to vaccination and immunization programs (section H.1) and the prevention of viral epidemics.
Relevant to aseptic and antiseptic precautions (section H.3) and prophylactic use of antibiotics.
Relevant to medical education (section A) and the broader educational frameworks within which healthcare professionals are trained.
Directly relevant to governmental practice and public health services (sections C.1.c, D), including health and safety regulations.
Relevant to the ethical basis of medical practice and health as a human right, including restrictions on child labour and workplace health protections.
Relevant to the practice of geriatric medicine and specialized care for aging populations within hospital and community settings.
Relevant to environmental sanitation and health (section F) and sustainable approaches to world food supply and pollution control.
